methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pasqal is a pre-IPO quantum computing company founded by Alain Aspect and Georges-Olivier Reymond that has raised €340M+. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across quantum computing: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Traditional peer-median valuation proves inapplicable here. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector comparables, but zero companies meeting narrow stage-alignment or valuation-tier criteria simultaneously. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of quantum approaches, commercialization timelines, and business model maturity across the peer set. This fundamental scarcity of tight comparables reflects the nascent state of the sector.

Without sufficient homogeneous peer data, triangulation via multiples analysis cannot be conducted with statistical confidence. A credible valuation range for Pasqal would require alternative methods—revenue-based models (if near-term revenue visibility exists), DCF analysis anchored to technology inflection timelines, or negotiated transaction evidence from similar-stage funding rounds. The primary valuation uncertainty remains the quantum computing sector's path to commercial viability and the wide dispersion in technical approaches among peers, which limits the applicability of relative valuation frameworks. Additional disclosures on Pasqal's revenue, customer pipeline, or recent funding terms would materially improve analytical precision.
